events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario describes reversed polarity on receptacles/switches in a residential job at rough-complete stage. Reversed polarity means hot and neutral are swapped. This is a non-compliant condition per NEC 406.4 (receptacle terminal connections) and potentially a safety hazard. Decision should be fail.

    "Receptacle(s) wired with reversed polarity — hot and neutral conductors are swapped at the device terminals.",
    "Reversed polarity creates a shock/energized-shell hazard on lamps and appliances and is non-compliant with NEC 406.4 terminal identification requirements."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize the affected circuit(s) and correct wiring so the black/hot conductor lands on the brass screw and the white/neutral lands on the silver screw.",
    "Verify polarity with a 3-light tester or multimeter after correction before closing out the wall.",
    "Inspect all roughed-in receptacles on the circuit for the same defect."
  ],
  "rationale": "At rough-complete, receptacles must be correctly wired per NEC 406.4. Reversed polarity is an installation defect and a code violation; the work cannot be closed out until corrected and verified.",
